commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=53d17ca7bf8a0cc16560c26d1d0ee2fc7c3e09fa)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shared Google Chat media-upload fallback helper and wraps monitor reply delivery, outbound adapter media sends, and message actions to send remote media URLs as text when attachment upload fails with an auth/scope error.

PR surface: Source +103, Tests +206. Total +309 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for a source-level reproduction: current main uses chat.bot app auth, and all three Google Chat media paths call the attachment upload endpoint before sending. I did not establish a live failing workspace run in this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs contributor real behavior proof and a small code fix or maintainer decision on the fallback predicate; ClawSweeper should not attempt repair automation while proof remains mock-only.

target.title: fix(googlechat): deliver media as a text link when app-auth upload 403s
target.body:
## Summary

Under Google Chat **app (bot)** authentication the OAuth scope is `chat.bot` (`extensions/googlechat/src/auth.ts`), which is not authorized for the attachment upload endpoint. `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` (`extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts`) therefore throws `Google Chat upload 403: ... PERMISSION_DENIED ... insufficient authentication scopes`.

All three outbound media paths swallowed that failure and dropped the media:

- `monitor-reply-delivery.ts` `sendMedia` only logged `Google Chat attachment send failed`
- `channel.adapters.ts` `attachedResults.sendMedia` had no `try`/`catch` (the error threw out)
- `actions.ts` `handleAction` had no `try`/`catch` (the error threw out)

so an agent replying with an image under app auth silently lost the message (impact:message-loss).

### Fix

A small shared module `media-upload-fallback.ts` exposes:

- `isAuthScopeUploadFailure(err)` — matches `403` / `PERMISSION_DENIED` / `insufficient authentication scopes`
- `isRemoteHttpMediaUrl(url)` — only remote `http(s)` media qualifies
- `buildMediaLinkFallbackText(text, url)` — appends the URL to the caption

Each upload call is wrapped so that **only** an auth/scope failure on a remote `http(s)` URL falls back to sending the URL as a normal text message. Local files (no public URL) and non-auth failures (size, network, malformed response) still surface unchanged. No config flag and no cardsV2 — the narrow remote-URL fallback the issue points at.

Fixes #89430

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Google Chat media replies under app (bot) auth were silently dropped because the `chat.bot` scope cannot upload attachments (403); they are now delivered as a text link instead of being lost.

**Real environment tested**: vitest (the repo's `extension-messaging` config) driving the three real exported send paths — `deliverGoogleChatReply`, `googlechatOutboundAdapter.attachedResults.sendMedia`, and `googlechatMessageActions.handleAction` — with `./api.js` mocked only at the HTTP boundary so `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` rejects with the real `Google Chat upload 403: {"error":{"status":"PERMISSION_DENIED","message":"Request had insufficient authentication scopes."}}` string.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**: `Test Files  3 passed (3)` / `Tests  32 passed (32)`, including the six new #89430 cases (one fallback + one negative-control per path). Reverting only the three source edits while keeping the tests flips exactly the three fallback cases to failing (`Tests  3 failed | 29 passed`), proving the tests bind the fix and the negative controls do not regress.

**Observed result after fix**: On a 403 upload with a remote `https` URL, each path calls `sendGoogleChatMessage` exactly once with `text` = caption + "\n" + URL and **no** attachments (the reply receipt's part kind is `text`); the message action returns `{ ok: true, mediaDelivery: "link-fallback" }`. Negative controls confirm a non-auth `500` upload error and a local-file (`/tmp/...`) upload still surface their error with no silent fallback.

**What was not tested**: A live Google Chat workspace round-trip — the upload endpoint's real 403 is reproduced via the exact error string raised by `api.ts`, not a real network call; cardsV2 image-widget delivery is intentionally out of scope per the issue's narrow-fallback direction.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: alix-007
